notices.title: Lehi City Council Meeting Agenda
notices.agenda_text:
Lehi City Council Meeting

Agenda

April 28, 2026

7:00 PM - Lehi City Council Chambers (131 N. 100 E., Lehi, Utah)

Regular Session, 7:00 p.m.

I.	Welcome, Roll Call, Pledge of Allegiance

II.	Presentations and Reports

1.	Dragonfly Park Presentation and Discussion

2.	Presentation and Discussion of Bike and Pedestrian Master Plan Vision, Goals, and Map. 

III.	Citizen Input

For public comments on items not listed on the agenda. Comments limited to 3 minutes per person, with a time limit not to exceed 20 minutes for this item.

IV.  Consent Agenda

1.	Approve Purchase Orders

2.	Approve City Council Meeting Minutes 

3.	Consideration of Resolution #2026-30, approving an agreement with SMM Excavation for the 2025 Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) Downtown Street Improvement Project.


4.	Consideration of Resolution #2026-31, approving the allocation of PARC Tax funds for cultural facilities organizations with Lehi City.

 
V.	Regular Agenda

1.	Public Hearing to consider feedback on the Lehi City Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) Annual Action Plan, which outlines community needs, potential community funding priorities, and program structure and governance for the program year 2026-2027.


2.	Cedar Hollow Holdings Annexation located at 400 South Center Street.

a.	Public Hearing

b.	Consideration of Resolution #2026-32 approving an Annexation Agreement.

c.	Consideration of Ordinance #22-2026 approving the Cedar Hollow Holdings Annexation, with R-1-Flex zoning on 15.31 acres of property located at approximately 400 South Center Street.
Petitioner: Gary Cooper


3.	Slay Holdings Annexation located at 2300 West 300 South

a.	Public Hearing

b.	Consideration of Resolution #2026-33 approving an Annexation Agreement.

c.	Consideration of Ordinance #23-2026 approving the Slay Holdings Annexation, with R-1-Fex (residential) zoning on 4 acres of property located at approximately 2300 West 300 South.
Petitioner: Slay Holdings


4.	Consideration of Ordinance #21-2026 creating the Lehi City Small Business Advisory Council and approving and adopting an accompanying Guiding Principles Policy. 

5.	Boards and Committees Update and Discussion

VI.  Administrative Report

VII. Mayor and Council Reports

Calendar items and outside Committee reports

Closed Session

The Council may consider a motion to enter into a closed session for specific purposes allowed under the Open and Public Meetings Act (Utah Code 52-4-205), including to discuss the purpose, exchange, lease, or sale of real property; litigation; the character, competence, or physical or mental health of an individual; or any other lawful purpose.

VIII. Adjournment




Public is invited to attend all open meetings


In compliance with the Americans with Disabilities Act, the City of Lehi will make reasonable accommodations to participate in the meeting. Requests for assistance can be made by contacting the City Recorder at 385-201-2269 This Agenda has been posted in accordance with Utah Code §§ 63G-30-102 Agendas may be amended up to 24 hours prior to a meeting

A Closed Meeting may be called to order pursuant to Utah Code §§ 52-4-204 & 52-4-205


The order of agenda items may change to accommodate the needs of the legislative body, staff, or the public Electronic participation may be permitted for one or more members of the legislative body and staff

notices.title: Cedar Hollow Annexation Public Hearing Notice
notices.agenda_text:
PUBLIC NOTICE

CEDAR HOLLOW HOLDINGS ANNEXATION
PUBLIC HEARING


On March 10, 2026, the Lehi City Council passed Resolution #2026-10 approving Lehi City's intent to annex approximately 15.31 acres of real property owned by Gary Cooper, located at 400 S. Center Street, Lehi, Utah. 
A public hearing has been scheduled before the Lehi City Council on the 28th day of April, 2026, at 7:00 p.m. for the purpose of receiving public comment.  The public hearing will be held at the Lehi Civic Center, in the Council Chambers, located at 131 N. 100 E., Lehi, UT.  
The Lehi City Council will annex the above described parcel unless, at or before the public hearing, written protests to the annexation are filed by the owners of real property located within the area proposed for annexation covering a majority of the total private land area within the entire area proposed for annexation and also is equal to at least 1/2 the assessed value of all private real property within the entire area proposed for annexation.
notices.meeting_location: 131 N 100 E Lehi, UT 84043 Show in Apple Maps Show in Google Maps

notices.title: Slay Holdings Annexation Public Hearing Notice
notices.agenda_text:
PUBLIC NOTICE

SLAY HOLDINGS ANNEXATION
PUBLIC HEARING


On March 10, 2026, the Lehi City Council passed Resolution #2026-09 approving Lehi City's intent to annex approximately 4 acres of real property owned by Slay Holdings LLC., located at approximately 2300 W. 300 S., Lehi, Utah. 
A public hearing has been scheduled before the Lehi City Council on the 28th day of April, 2026, at 7:00 p.m. for the purpose of receiving public comment.  The public hearing will be held at the Lehi Civic Center, in the Council Chambers, located at 131 N. 100 E., Lehi, UT.  
The Lehi City Council will annex the above described parcel unless, at or before the public hearing, written protests to the annexation are filed by the owners of real property located within the area proposed for annexation covering a majority of the total private land area within the entire area proposed for annexation and also is equal to at least 1/2 the assessed value of all private real property within the entire area proposed for annexation.

notices.title: Public Hearing Notice for the Lehi City Community Development Block Grant
notices.agenda_text:
Public Hearing and Public Comment Period Notice
Lehi City Community Development Block Grant
Mountainland Association of Governments will hold a public hearing to consider feedback on the Lehi City CDBG Annual Action Plan, which outlines community needs, potential community funding priorities, and program structure and governance for the program year 2026-2027. The amount of CDBG funds for this program year will be discussed along with the range of projects eligible.
The hearing will be held in the Lehi Council Chambers located at 131 North 100 East, Lehi, Utah, on April 28th at 7:00 pm. Suggestions will be solicited verbally, electronically, and in writing from all interested parties during the public comment period from April 3 to May 5, 2026. To comment or for further information, contact Claudia Saldana at 801-229-3673 or csaldana@mountainland.org.
The public hearing facility is fully accessible to persons with disabilities. Individuals needing special accommodations (including auxiliary aids/services or translation) during the hearing should notify Claudia Saldana at least five days prior to the hearing. A Utah Relay telephone number or a language interpreter can be provided upon request.
________________________________________
Mountainland Association of Governments llevará a cabo una audiencia pública para considerar la retroalimentación sobre el Plan de Acción Anual de la Ciudad de Lehi CDBG, que resume las necesidades de la comunidad, posibles prioridades de financiación comunitaria y la estructura y gestión del programa para el año 2026-2027. El monto estimado de los fondos CDBG para este año del programa se examinará junto con la gama de proyectos elegibles.
La audiencia se llevará a cabo en las oficinas de la ciudad de Lehi, 131 North 100 East, Lehi, Utah, el Martes 28 de Abril de 2026 a las 7:00 pm. Las sugerencias serán solicitadas verbalmente, electrónicamente y por escrito de todas las partes interesadas durante el período de comentarios públicos del 3 de abril al 5 de mayo de 2026. Para comentar u obtener información adicional, póngase en contacto con Claudia Saldaña al 801-229-3673 o csaldana@mountainland.org.
La instalación de la audiencia pública es totalmente accesible para personas con discapacidad. Las personas que necesiten adaptaciones especiales (servicios auxiliares o traducción) durante la audiencia deberán notificar a Claudia Saldana al menos cinco días antes de la misma. Un número de teléfono de Utah Relay o un intérprete de idiomas pueden estar disponibles bajo petición.

notices.title: Lehi City Budget Amendment Notice of Public Hearing
notices.agenda_text:
APRIL 7, 2026


Public Hearing Notice
Lehi City 

Notice is hereby given that the Lehi City Council will conduct a public hearing on Tuesday, April 14, 2026, which begins at 7:00 p.m., at Lehi City Civic Center, in the City Council Chambers, 131 N 100 E, for the purpose of receiving public input regarding an amendment to the Lehi City FY2026 Budget.  Public comments are invited.

Lehi City Council Meeting

Agenda

March 10, 2026

Lehi City Council Chambers (153 N. 100 E., Lehi, Utah)

V.	Administrative Report

Closed Meeting

The Council may consider a motion to enter into a closed session for specific purposes allowed under the Open and Public Meetings Act (Utah Code 52-4-205), including to discuss the purpose, exchange,
 
lease, or sale of real property; litigation; the character, competence, or physical or mental health of an individual; or any other lawful purpose.

Regular Session, 7:00 p.m.

XI.  Regular Agenda

1.	Consideration of Resolution #2026-13, approving an agreement with UDOT for the design, construction and maintenance of a pedestrian bridge over the Jordan River as part of the 2100 N Freeway Project.


2.	Consideration of Resolution #2026-14, approving an agreement with UDOT for design, construction, and maintenance of a shared use path as part of the 2100 N Freeway Project.

acres located at 300 North Palomino Way changing the zoning from A-1 (agriculture) to R-1-22 (residential/agriculture).

Petitioner: Grow Development


8.	Consideration of Ordinance #07-2026, approving the Snow Zone Change on 0.66 acres located at 151 East 1450 North changing the zoning from TH-5 (transitional holding) to R-1-Flex (residential).

Petitioner: Lawrence Snow 

9.	Consideration of Ordinance #08-2026, approving the 498 N 900 E Zone Change on .91-acres located at 498 North 900 East changing the zoning from R-1-Flex (residential) to R-1-8 (residential).

Petitioner: Lehi City 

10.	Consideration of Ordinance #09-2026, approving a Development Code amendment to Chapter 5, Table of Uses (nonresidential) and Chapter 39 to add Automobile Accessories and Installation.
